title: Mechanical circulatory support device with axial flow turbomachine optimized for heart failure and cardio-renal syndrome
abstract: Mechanical circulatory supports configured to operate in series with the native heart are disclosed. In an embodiment, an intravascular propeller is installed into the descending aorta and anchored within via an expandable anchoring mechanism. The propeller and anchoring mechanism may be foldable so as to be percutaneously deliverable to the aorta. The propeller may have foldable blades. The blades may be magnetic and may be driven by a concentric electromagnetic stator circumferentially outside the magnetic blades. The stator may be intravascular or may be configured to be installed around the outer circumference of the blood vessel. The support may create a pressure rise between about 20-50 mmHg, and maintain a flow rate of about 5 L/min. The support may have one or more pairs of contra- rotating propellers to modulate the tangential velocity of the blood flow. The support may have static pre-swirlers and or de-swirlers. The support may be optimized to replicate naturally occurring vortex formation within the descending aorta.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A mechanical circulatory support for assisting the heart, the support comprising: at least one propeller, the at least one propeller comprising a plurality of blades arranged around an axis of rotation, the blades being configured to pump blood in a substantially axial direction parallel to the axis of rotation, wherein at least one of the plurality of blades is magnetic; a shaft aligned along the axis of rotation of the at least one propeller; an anchoring mechanism, the anchoring mechanism being configured to anchor the at least one propeller within a lumen of a blood vessel, the anchoring mechanism comprising: a proximal hub coupled to a proximal end of the shaft; a distal hub coupled to a distal end of the shaft; a collapsed configuration for installing the anchoring mechanism in the blood vessel; and an expanded configuration wherein at least a portion of the anchoring mechanism is configured to be pressed against a wall of the lumen of the blood vessel; and at least one ring-shaped stator comprising one or more electromagnets positioned around the circumference of the stator, wherein the at least one stator is configured to be positioned concentrically around the blades of the at least one propeller to electromagnetically drive rotation of the at least one magnetic blade. 2. The support of claim 1, wherein all of the blades of the at least one propeller are configured to be foldable substantially along the shaft such that in the collapsed configuration of the anchoring mechanism the blades are in a folded position, wherein the collapsed configuration is configured for percutaneously installing the anchoring mechanism in the blood vessel through a catheter. 3. The support of claim 1 or 2, wherein the at least one propeller comprises a pair of contra-rotating propellers configured to rotate in opposite directions. 4. The support of any one of the preceding claims, the support further comprises a plurality of stationary de-s wirier vanes coupled to either the shaft or the anchoring 

 mechanism, the de-swirler vanes being positioned downstream of the at least one propeller and being configured to remove or reduce a tangential velocity component of blood flow as it leaves the support. 5. The support of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a plurality of stationary pre-swirler vanes coupled to either the shaft or the anchoring mechanism, the pre-swirler vanes being positioned upstream of the at least one propeller and being configured to increase a tangential velocity component of blood flow entering the support. 6. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one stator is configured to be positioned around an outer circumference of the blood vessel. 7. The support of claim 6, wherein the at least one stator comprises a hinge configured to allow the stator to open and close, the stator having a circumference and being configured to open along the circumference for positioning the stator around the blood vessel and to close for securing the stator around the outer circumference of the blood vessel. 8. The support of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the at least one stator is configured to be positioned along an inner circumference of the lumen of the blood vessel. 9. The support of claim 8, wherein the at least one stator comprises a collapsed configuration for percutaneous delivery via a catheter and an expanded configuration. 10. The support of claim 8 or 9, wherein the at least one stator is coupled to or integral with the anchoring mechanism. 11. The support of claim 9, wherein the at least one stator comprises first and second discrete ring-shaped components, the first and second discrete ring-shaped components each comprising circumferentially offset electromagnets, wherein the electromagnets of the second discrete ring-shaped component are configured to be positioned circumferentially between the electromagnets of the first discrete -ring shaped component. 12. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one propeller comprises a plurality of propellers configured to rotate together. 13. The support of claim 12, wherein at least one propeller does not comprise any magnetic blades. 14. The support of claim 12, wherein all the blades of all the propellers are magnetic. 15. The support of one of claims 12 to 14, wherein a radial tip of at least one blade from each propeller is connected via a magnetic connector extending substantially along an outer diameter of the plurality of propellers. 16. The support of any one of claims 12 to 15, wherein the at least one ring- shaped stator comprises a plurality of ring shaped stators, each stator being axially aligned with one of the plurality of propellers. 17. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one magnetic blade comprises a magnet positioned within or coupled to a radial tip of the blade. 18. The support of claim 17, wherein the at least one magnetic blade comprises a magnetic winglet coupled to the radial tip of the blade. 19. The support of claim 17, wherein the at least one magnetic blade comprises a magnetic ring coupled to the radial tip of the blade, the magnetic ring joining plurality of blades of the at least one propeller. 20. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one magnetic blade is formed from a magnetic material. 21. The support of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a ferrous ring configured to be placed in the blood vessel between the propellers and the blood vessel wall. 22. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one propeller is configured to rotate around the shaft, a bearing being positioned between the shaft and the at least one propeller. 23. The support of any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein the shaft is configured to rotate with the at least one propeller, a bearing being positioned between the shaft and the proximal hub and a bearing being positioned between the shaft and the distal hub. 24. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the blades are deformable so as to be foldable toward the shaft. 25. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the support comprises a partially disassembled configuration and a fully assembled configuration, 

 wherein the propeller comprises a channel for receiving the shaft and the distal hub comprises a first mechanical feature for coupling to a second mechanical feature on the shaft, the shaft being fixedly coupled to the proximal hub, wherein the shaft, proximal hub, and distal hub are not rigidly secured together in the partially disassembled configuration, wherein a tensioning line connects the shaft and the distal hub in the partially disassembled configuration, the tensioning line extending through the propeller channel, and wherein applying tension to the tensioning line places the support in the fully assembled configuration, wherein in the fully assembled configuration the shaft extends through the propeller channel and the first mechanical feature and the second mechanical feature are coupled together rigidly securing the shaft, proximal hub, and distal hub together, and wherein the plurality of blades are configured to extend in a substantially perpendicular direction to the shaft in the assembled configuration. 26. The support of any one of claims 2 to 25, wherein the at least one propeller comprises two blades, the blades being foldable along the shaft in opposite directions. 27. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the proximal hub is adjustably displaceable along the shaft such that the proximal hub can be moved closer to the distal hub to place the anchoring mechanism in an expanded configuration and/or the proximal hub can be moved further from the distal hub to place the anchoring mechanism in a collapsed configuration. 28. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the anchoring mechanism comprises a proximal half and a distal half, the proximal half of the anchoring mechanism being separate or separable from the distal half of the anchoring mechanism, and wherein the shaft comprises a proximal half and a distal half, the proximal half of the shaft being separable from and attachable to the distal half of the shaft. 29. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the shaft comprises a plurality of joints dividing the shaft into at least three foldable portions, the shaft being in a straightened configuration when the 

 foldable portions are aligned along the axis of rotation and the shaft being in a folded configuration when the foldable portions are folded, and wherein the at least one propeller is coupled to a foldable portion positioned between the most proximal fordable portion and the most distal foldable portion of the shaft such that the plurality of blades of the at least one propeller may be aligned substantially parallel to the most proximal foldable portion and the most distal foldable portion in the folded configuration. 30. The support of claim 29, wherein the shaft comprises two joints configured to allow the shaft to assume a z-shape configuration in the folded configuration. 31. The support of claim 29, wherein the shaft comprises four joints configured to allow the shaft to assume a c-shape configuration in the folded configuration. 32. The support of any one of claims 29-31, further comprising a securing shaft configured to be inserted through an internal lumen of the shaft to lock the shaft into a straightened configuration. 33. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the anchoring mechanism comprises a plurality of leaflet springs coupled to the propeller, the leaflet springs being configured to extend in a radially outward direction from the propeller to contact the blood vessel wall and anchor the propeller within the blood vessel, and wherein the leaflet springs comprise a deformed configuration configured to allow the anchoring mechanism to be compressed for percutaneous delivery via a catheter. 34. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the anchoring mechanism is configured to be installed in the descending aorta and wherein the support is configured to provide a pressure rise between about 20 mmHg and about 50 mmHg in the blood flow and to maintain a flow rate of about 5 L/min. 35. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the support is configured to produce a right handed helical blood flow comprising a vorticity about equal to that of the native descending aorta at an output of the support. 36. The support of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the anchoring mechanism comprises a plurality of struts extending between the proximal hub and the distal hub, the struts being bendable or flexible. 37. A method of treating congestive heart failure in a patient, the method comprising: installing a mechanical circulation support within the lumen of the descending aorta of the patient, wherein the mechanical circulation support comprises: at least one propeller, the at least one propeller comprising a plurality of blades arranged around an axis of rotation, the blades being configured to pump blood in a substantially axial direction parallel to the axis of rotation, wherein at least one of the plurality of blades is magnetic; a shaft aligned along the axis of rotation of the at least one propeller; an anchoring mechanism, the anchoring mechanism being configured to anchor the at least one propeller within the lumen, the anchoring mechanism comprising: a proximal hub coupled to a proximal end of the shaft; a distal hub coupled to a distal end of the shaft; a collapsed configuration for installing the anchoring mechanism in the descending aorta; and an expanded configuration wherein at least a portion of the anchoring mechanism is configured to be pressed against a wall of the lumen of the descending aorta; and at least one ring-shaped stator comprising one or more electromagnets positioned around the circumference of the stator, wherein the at least one stator is configured to be positioned concentrically around the blades of the at least one propeller to electromagnetically drive rotation of the at least one magnetic blade. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein the support is configured to provide a pressure rise between about 20 mmHg and about 50 mmHg in the blood flow and to maintain a flow rate of about 5 L/min. 39. The method of claim 37 or 38, wherein installing the support comprises percutaneously installing the rotor and the anchoring mechanism in the lumen through a catheter, the anchoring mechanism assuming the collapsed configuration during delivery, and 

 expanding the anchoring mechanism into an expanded configuration such that the anchoring mechanism anchors the rotor within the lumen. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein installing the support further comprises percutaneously installing the at least one stator in the lumen through a catheter. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein the at least one stator is coupled to the anchoring mechanism. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein the at least one stator is installed prior to the anchoring mechanism. 43. The method of claim 42, wherein the at least one stator comprises first and second discrete ring-shaped components, the first and second discrete ring-shaped components each comprising circumferentially offset electromagnets, the installing the at least one stator comprising installing the first discrete ring-shaped component and subsequently installing the second discrete ring shaped component so that the electromagnets of the second discrete ring-shaped component are positioned circumferentially between the electromagnets of the first discrete-ring shaped component. 44. The method of any one of claims 37 to 39, wherein installing the support further comprises surgically installing the at least one stator around an outer circumference of the descending aorta such that the at least one stator is axially aligned with the at least one propeller. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the at least one stator comprises a hinge allowing the stator to assume an open configuration and a closed configuration, wherein installing the stator comprises positioning the stator around the descending aorta in an open configuration and closing the stator. 46. The method of claim 37 or 38, wherein installing the support comprises making a surgical incision in the descending aorta and installing the anchoring mechanism into the lumen through the incision. 47. The method of any one of claims 37 to 46, wherein the patient has stage II or stage III congestive heart failure. 48. The method of claim 47, wherein the patient has late stage II or early stage III congestive heart failure. 49. A mechanical circulatory support for assisting the heart, the support comprising: at least one propeller, the at least one propeller comprising a plurality of blades arranged around an axis of rotation, the blades being configured to pump blood in a substantially axial direction parallel to the axis of rotation, wherein at least one of the plurality of blades is magnetic; a shaft aligned along the axis of rotation of the at least one propeller; an anchoring mechanism, the anchoring mechanism being configured to anchor the at least one propeller within a lumen of a blood vessel, the anchoring mechanism comprising: a proximal hub coupled to a proximal end of the shaft; a distal hub coupled to a distal end of the shaft; a collapsed configuration for installing the anchoring mechanism in the blood vessel; and an expanded configuration wherein at least a portion of the anchoring mechanism is configured to be pressed against a wall of the lumen of the blood vessel; and at least one motor configured to drive rotation of the at least one propeller. 50. The support of claim 49, wherein all of the blades of the at least one propeller are configured to be foldable substantially along the shaft such that in the collapsed configuration of the anchoring mechanism the blades are in a folded position, wherein the collapsed configuration is configured for percutaneously installing the anchoring mechanism in the blood vessel through a catheter. 51. The support of claim 49 or 50, wherein the at least one propeller comprises a pair of contra-rotating propellers configured to rotate in opposite directions. 52. The support of any one of claims 49 to 51, the support further comprises a plurality of stationary de-swirler vanes coupled to either the shaft or the anchoring mechanism, the de-swirler vanes being positioned downstream of the at least one propeller and being configured to remove or reduce a tangential velocity component of blood flow as it leaves the support. 53. The support of any one of claims 49 to 52, further comprising a plurality of stationary pre-swirler vanes coupled to either the shaft or the anchoring mechanism, the pre- swirler vanes being positioned upstream of the at least one propeller and being configured to increase a tangential velocity component of blood flow entering the support. 54. The support of any one of claims 49 to 53, wherein the at least one motor is configured to be extra-corporeal, the motor being configured to drive rotation of the propeller via a driveline percutaneously extending through the body of a patient and connecting the motor to the shaft. 55. The support of any one of claims 49 to 53, wherein the at least one motor is configured to be positioned within the lumen of the blood vessel, the motor being configured to rotate the shaft to drive rotation of the propeller. 56. The support of claim 55, wherein the at least one motor comprises a plurality of motors configured to be positioned within the lumen and the at least one propeller comprises a plurality of propellers, wherein each motor is configured to drive rotation of one of the plurality of propellers. 57. The support of any one of claims 49 to 55, wherein the at least one propeller comprises a pair of contra-rotating propellers which are mechanically connected and the at least one motor comprises a single motor configured to drive the pair of contra-rotating propellers in opposite directions.
